approach: DR02 — repo operations, paths, provenance, claude.ai pack

findings.finding: Canonical path drift must be gated
Batch / provenance parity must be hard-gated
claude.ai pack must be a small text-only operational pack, not giant JSONL
findings.verification_method: verify_claude_ai_pack.py PASS (28 files, 139,983 B, <5MB, no file >500KB)

findings.finding: Corpus fixture must be read-only (no live write, no translation copy)
examples[].en must be qamus-authored public text, NOT a copied translation — is it covered by a validator, or a policy gap?
findings.remaining_action: OWNER DECISION: confirm Saheeh-Intl redistribution rights+attribute / replace with open-licensed / author original / drop en from public dataset. NOTE: separate from hover output (clean).
findings.verification_method: audit_examples_en_provenance.py: 7,700 examples, 41.3% carry Saheeh-International [bracket] signature; dataset validator gates structure/leak/private but NOT anti-copy
live words scan: 0 informed_by/qac/tanzil/ocr keys; ALLOWED_* + PRIVATE_FIELDS + LEAK_PAT gate every dataset level

head: d67f873
summary: Canonical-path hygiene, batch/provenance parity, the claude.ai pack, and the corpus fixture are all validator-gated and PASS. The hover output is leak-free (0 private keys live + structural gating at every dataset level). **The decisive DR02 finding: `examples[].en` is NOT qamus-authored — it is verbatim Saheeh International** (7,700 examples, 41.3% carry the `[bracket]` interpolation signature), copied into the PUBLIC dataset. The dataset validator gates structure/leak/private keys but has no anti-copy guard. Built `audit_examples_en_provenance.py` to quantify it. **Classification: owner-gated (source/licensing).** It blocks public-repo redistribution cleanliness only — NOT coverage-to-90 and NOT the (clean) hover output.
